analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet highlights an EU collaboration with Cartooning for Peace and an AU calendar designer to promote freedom of expression and the AU–EU partnership through visuals during the AU Year of Arts & Culture. Evaluation: EU1 (EU is a successful story) – Not invoked; no reference to EU history or overcoming challenges. EU2 (EU is united) – Not addressed; no mention of intra-EU unity or solidarity. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens) – Not applicable; focus is external and on advocacy, not on EU-internal benefits. EU4 (EU as avant‑garde political actor) – Not clearly; while promoting expression and arts, it does not present pioneering regulation or leadership on future challenges. EU5 (EU a global champion of fair causes) – Yes; it explicitly states the EU believes cartoons strengthen freedom of expression, positioning the EU as defending a human right. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – Yes; it promotes AU–EU partnership and cultural cooperation, suggesting the EU’s constructive role abroad and in multilateral cooperation. Conclusion: EU5 and EU6 are promoted.
